title: TH17 cells contribute to uveitis and scleritis and are inhibited by IL-27/STAT1 in the retina. (2) Expression of IL-17 in mouse PBMC, lymph node and retina
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dn3yf","text":"Cheng-Rong Yu prepared this manuscript and should have been the first author.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ugh3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"q4fnp","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pzpg1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sibtw","text":"Expression of IL-17 in mouse PBMC, lymph node and retina is temporally correlated with progression of experimental autoimmune uveoretinitis (EAU)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xf6wz","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nvqpb","text":"Uveitis is a diverse group of intraocular inflammatory diseases that cause severe visual loss and morbidity and is characterized by inflammatory attack of the uvea as well as the neuroretina 1. The disease may be of infectious or putative autoimmune etiology. The latter include birdshot retinochoroidopathy, sympathetic ophthalmia, Behçet’s disease, Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome, and ocular sarcoidosis 1. Understanding the immunopathogenic mechanisms of uveitis has benefited enormously by the development of an animal model of uveitis, experimental autoimmune uveitis (EAU). EAU is a predominantly a T cell-mediated intraocular inflammatory disease induced in susceptible species by active immunization with ocular-specific proteins (or peptides derived from them) and is transferable to naive syngeneic animals by injection of in vitro-activated CD4+, MHC class II-restricted T cell lines specific to retinal Ags 2-4. The two major uveitogenic retinal proteins are S-Ag (also termed “arrestin”) and interphotoreceptor retinoid-binding protein (IRBP) 2-4. In this study, we have used the EAU mouse model to further establish that TH17 cells are involved in pathogenic mechanisms of uveitis by showing that EAU can be ameliorated by treatment with antibody to IL-17. steps: EAU was induced with 150μg bovine IRBP and 300μg human IRBP peptide (1-20) in C57bl/6 strain. Disease was induced in B10RIII mice with 150μg bovine IRBP and 12-20 μg human IRBP peptide (161-180) and clinical disease in both strains were established by fundoscopy and histology. Initial signs EAU is observed approximately 7-12 days after immunization, with full-blown disease developing by post-immunization day-14. By day-21 post-immunization there is substantial reduction of inflammatory cells and disease resolution characterized by absence of inflammatory cells or inflammatory cytokines in the retina occurs 28 days post-immunization 6 .
Into a 50 ml polypropylene tube, add 0.5 ml stock bovine IRBP protein (6 mg/ml) and 6 mg lyophilized human IRBP peptide (1-20) and bring mixture to a final volume of 2 ml
Add an equal volume or 2 ml of Complete Freund adjuvant (CFA) containing Mycobacterium tuberculosis strain H37Ra (2.5 mg/ml), mix and chill on ice for 10 min
Sonicate at high speed on ice for 20 seconds followed by 30 seconds intervals
Repeat 4-5 times until the mixture appears as a thick white cream.
Centrifuge at 1200 RPM for 1 min.
Prepare CFA emulsion without IRBP to serve as control.
Transfer emulsion into in 1 ml syringes with 19 gauge blunt-ended needles
Prepare PTX (2 ug/ml) solution in 1 ml syringes with 25 gauge needles
Prepare sufficient PTX (2 ug/ml) solution (100μl/mouse)
Prepare sufficient CFA/Antigen emulsions (200μl/mouse)
Before injections expel any air bubble in the syringe containing PTX or emulsion
Inject each mouse with PTX solution (100 μl) intraperitoneally
Inject emulsion subcutaneously into base of tail (100 μl) and each thigh (50 μl)
Sacrifice the immunized at post-immunization day-7, -14, -21, and -28. Controls: Un-immunized WT mice; mice immunized with CFA emulsion (without IRBP)
Restrain mice by anethesia: intraperitoneal injection of mixture of Ketamine and xylazine (Rompun) at 30 mg/kg and 9 mg/kg, respectively.
After 5-10 min, dilate pupils by applying 1 drop of Mydriacyl (1%) to each eye.
Gently press the cover glass on the cornea.
Adjust head position to focus retina to the fundoscope for examination
Note: Mydriacyl causes temporary lens opacification within 15-20 min after application; it is important to complete examination within this time.
Incubate enucleated eyes overnight in 10% buffered formaldehyde
Embed eyes and cut 5μM paraffin sections in anterior/posterior orientation.
Stain sections in hematoxylin & eosin (H&E).
Grading of EAU pathology is as previously described.
Active immunization of C57BL/6 mice with IRBP in CFA
Dilute IL-17 or IgD2a isotype control antibodies in PBS
Inject 500 μl IL-17 antibody (350 μg) intraperitoreally into treatment group (4 mice) 1 day before immunization with IRBP/CFA emulsion (day -1).
Inject 500 μl IgD2a isotype control antibody (350 μg) intraperitoreally into control group (4 mice) 1 day before immunization with IRBP/CFA emulsion (day -1).
Repeat Ab injection every other day until day 17 post-immunization.
Perform fundscopic examinations at post-immunization days 12, 14 and 16.
Sacrifice mice at post-immunization day-19 and perform histology
Immunise mice (8) with CFA or IRBP/CFA emulsion
Monitor EAU progression by fundoscopy and histology
Sacrifice mice at post-immunization days, 0, 7, 14, 21 and 28
Section 1 eye for histology and stain with H&E
Isolate RNA and protein extracts from retina, PBMC or lymph nodes
Analyze IL-27 protein expression by Western blotting
Analyze IL27 and Ebi3 expression by RT-PCR and qPCR.

TH17 cells are expanded by IL-2 during Uveitis or Scleritis and inhibited by IL-27/STAT1 pathways by Ahjoku Amadi-Obi, Cheng-Rong Yu, Xuebin Liu, Rashid M Mahdi, Grace Levy Clarke, Robert B Nussenblatt, Igal Gery, Yun Sang Lee & Charles E Egwuagu published in Nature Medicine on 17 Aug, 2007 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nm1585
creator: Protocols Importer
